name: Silent Slayer: Vault of the Vampire

reviews.review_description: Now, let me get one thing clear. If you’re looking for a game that’s like Schell’s other games, this is not it. HOWEVER, that does NOT mean that this is not an amazing game. If you are a fan of some good old fashioned scares, this is absolutely for you. I will admit that I am not the kind of person who usually plays horror games, and by that I mean that when I first tried this game, at the first jump scare, I screamed, stumbled backwards, tripped over a laundry basket, slammed aggressively into the floor, and effectively whapped myself in the face with the already flailing controllers. It was honestly a five star introduction to the game if you ask me. My whole family thinks I’m insane for finding the joy that I do in this game, but you gotta love that adrenaline rush. Highly, highly recommend to anyone looking for a good horror game.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your detailed feedback - we're so glad to hear you're enjoying the game!

reviews.review_description: My hands shake to much for this game. Its a cool game tho

A tense and fun game that could really only be done in VR. Quietly and quickly disarm the traps and alarms of sleeping vampires' coffins to drive a stake into their hearts before they wake and strike first. More than a simple jumpscare simulator, there's a story and the defenses on the coffins get more intricate the further you progress through the lairs of the undead. It's stationary and can be played in mixed reality and just the right balance between making me nervous while making sure I'm still having fun.
Amazing game! Please please add more levels/DLC! please. I can play this game forever.

Great game. Only downside is, that it is short, but everything there is well implemented.
I hope for expansion or new game by the same team.

Silent Slayer: Vault of the Vampire works wonderfully as both an introduction to VR Horror as well as a fun game to help showcase a VR Headset to friends and family alike.

The puzzles are simple once you get the hang of them, the environments have an air of creepiness and unease throughout them, and the game allows you to replay any chapter and specific segments almost instantly. Plus, you can turn off the spider in the settings.

The only thing I feel is currently missing from the game is Voice Immersion. Having to be silent, both in-game and real life would be extremely funny. Aside from that, its a wonderful 2-3 hour experience with the flexibility to replay chapters and segments on the fly.
yeah don't waste your money every time i try to play it it doesnt work

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you're encountering difficulties with Silent Slayer. Please reach out to our player support team at bit.ly/4aR5Wmr so we can help you work through the issue.

reviews.review_description: The game is very sensitive to movement Like extremely but really fun It took a while to beat it even a year to beat one level Because of the sensitivity, but I beat it a couple days ago And it was really fun, loved the design of every single bit.It's a masterpiece, and I love it to every bit of it, except for the sensitivity But that's okay  vampire lovers puzzle lovers gothic horror lovers  or just people who like scary games with scary jump scares and scary ambiences will probably love this i love it and I not into any of those thingsI just like it because it's fun it's amazing to get up close with a vampire love this game Love the detail  love everything except for the sensitivity but it is really really good five out of five stars amazing 👍

reviews.review_title: I can’t even stab the vampire
reviews.review_description: I want my money back

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you're having trouble with the game. Every vampire is different, so various techniques are important depending on which vampire you're having trouble with.  Please reach out to our player support team at bit.ly/4aR5Wmr and we'd be happy to give you tips to help you work through a level that is giving you trouble.

reviews.review_title: Get it now ! Get it now!
reviews.review_description: This game is good. If you like puzzles and you love vampires, this is like the perfect blend. I’d buy it three times over I recommend it to friends and family. Just don’t let the little ones play. It’s a little scary. But for a good time, definitely. Especially with cold weather and can’t be outside. Be inside having a scary good time.

reviews.review_title: Fun game that could use a few tweaks
reviews.review_description:
Silent Slayer: Vault of the Vampire is a fun puzzle game with some entertaining mechanics, a great sense of atmosphere and a few quirks that can get irritating after a while.

First and foremost the premise and gameplay is great, a perfect use of VR's strengths such as immersion and satisfying hand manipulations.

There are a few things that bother me a bit though:

1. When using tools such as the pry bar or the snippers, it would feel better and more natural to use the trigger button on the controller rather than the X or A button to perform the action.

2. When holding/using an object or opening the coffin, your hand disappears which is always a bit immersion breaking to me.

3. When snipping the wires that can criss-cross in front of the vampire, the stereoscopic effect sometimes seems to be off a bit, causing me to accidentally touch the wire instead.

4. After the 4th or 5th vampire, finding the sweet spot with the heart becomes much, much harder and it seems the slightest slip up wakes the vampire. I understand the need to gradually make each encounter harder but I think the curve could be a bit less steep (or maybe introduce difficulty levels).

Regardless, this is a fun game and I'm looking forward to getting back to it and sinking my teeth into it (haha).

A few DLCs with classic vampires like Bela Lugosi or Christopher Lee might be fun to explore down the road.
This was great! Love the mechanics of it, the story, the book’s voice, all the creepy sounds, the graphics, and the skill puzzles.￼ So much fun. I love Schell games! More, more, want more!
Nais
Just a skill game of doing things boringly slow. Not a single interesting puzzle.
Brak j. Polskiego

I beat this game in about a day and a half and I really enjoyed it. The only thing that would have made this game better is if it was longer. The narration was really good and the puzzles were challenging but still a lot of fun. I really recommend this game to anyone that's looking for a fun puzzle game.

If they made a sequel, here's what I'm hoping they'd add. 
1) Exploration. Before challenging a vampire, you can explore the castle to either find tools, weapons, and tips about how to kill each vampire. You'd have to solve puzzles to get the additional info and tools.
2) Tools could include oil to place on hinges to stop squeaking, a cross to give an additional try in case the vampire wakes up, garlic to keep the vampire asleep longer, etc.
3) A super hard secret boss that you'd have to figure out how to kill by piecing together clues from the castle. You'd have to select the tools to bring on your hunt and choosing the wrong tools you won't be able to defeat the boss. 
4) Additional types of puzzles to open coffins on harder vampires.
I love killing vampires 5 stars

Very good game. I actually liked it more than I Expect You to Die (only played the first game in the series so far). I think the "try not to die and repeat" loop works better in this more precision based gameplay.

My biggest complaint and the reason I'm not giving the game 5 stars are just one type of puzzles you have to solve before entering vampyre vaults. Puzzles are cool and all, but they get a bit repetitive.

I also kind of wish the game would be a bit more ambitious and let you actually explore the castle.

Update... I have just returned to this after 6 months or so, and the annoying mechanics are still there. 
Why can't you use the trigger buttons for something? It feels feels really natural to use the A or X buttons for the scissors or the nail lever. 
And following the binding arrows can be really  hit or miss. 
Overall, this is a cool game let down by bad mechanics. 

My advise, re map the controllers in the Quest settings.


It is a clever idea and the passthrough is cool addition.
However, I find the binding mechanics to be a bit frustrating. 
I find it best to take a few steps back before attempting them with just the tip of the stake. 
On the later levels, you can only grab the stake underhand, like stabbing a stomach, not overhand like you would plunge a stake into the heart. This feels awkward to me. 
Also, it would be better and feel more natural to use the trigger button with the nail tool and the scissors instead of the A/X buttons. 
I am not sure why the hands disappear when removing the nails? This feels strange and I feel like it would be better to see the nail between your fingers.

So unique, cleverly designed, I love these types of VR experiences where the focus is atmosphere and puzzle. 

As much as I love action heavy games, it's nice to have a game that's not exactly a wind down game like puzzling places, but like a true sit down game with pretty thoughtful kinetic movement. 

If I could change anything I just wish there was more. Maybe a leaderboard for speed, or a challenge mode. Optional coffins/vamps.

The Game Was amazing, I Already made it to the final Boss... though, When I Tried Opening it to Resume it, There's a Bug In Opening the Game.  If there is a Way to Resolve this issue, I Would Very Much Appreciate It.

This is advertised as a kind of escape room with jumpscares, but most of your time will be spent assembling puzzles before you get to the actual levels.  The Bindstones assembly puzzles are only there to raise the playing time as the game has 12 very similar levels, half of which are a breeze to complete. Having to suffer through some 3D spatial IQ test just to get to the actual levels is ridiculous. Between that and missing simple QOL elements like skipping the (tedious) story and no allowing players to replay previous levels makes this game not worth it. You cannot show the game to friends without showing them your skills at 3D spatial awareness for 20 minutes first.
It's very intertaining and I hope they make a sequel.

reviews.review_title: Not at all what I expected
reviews.review_description: For some reason, I thought I’d be chasing vampires instead of having to solve different puzzles to kill them in their crypts. Not really what I enjoy doing.

**Warning: This game is NOT for the faint of heart, or the impatient.**

This game is amazing! The ambience, structure, characters, and overall mechanics are exceptional. As a horror game, it's predictably terrifying. This game will get your heart racing to the point where you might feel like throwing up 😂. As of writing this, I've only completed the first two levels, and I'm already traumatized 😂. Please take this game slow, and/or take breaks, or you may feel sick.
I’m having a hard time  especially with the nails  please add an easier mode
It really is Milton Bradley's Operation.  Except when you fail you don't get a buzzer. You poop yourself.
Again a great game
Brilliant game to play, it would be a 5 ⭐️ if it didn’t keep stopping the game after 1 minute of play, worked fine until chapter 4

developer_response.body: It sounds like something isn't working quite right. Please reach out to our Player Support team at schellgames.com/support so we can help you work through the issue.

reviews.review_title: Great game! Only wished it was longer
reviews.review_description:
The suspense is killing you, as you use dexterity  and wit to clear out all the obstacles in your path to slay the vampire ( and get jumpscared along the way). On level 5 now , with 5 vampires left to go. The pace at which new elements are introduced is nice, increasing the complexity of the puzzles with each new vampire. 

Unique and compelling environment,  amazing backstory, by using an mysterious talking book that narrates along ( props to the voice actor) , with a each vampire having its own backstory. 

The only gripe, I have, is that I wished it was longer ( beyond 10 vampires ) and had a bit more replay value. 

Maybe after finishing the original game, unlocking a career/leaderboard mode  as a slayer, with randomly generated puzzles (using the same vampire models and all the tools at your disposal , just randomized traps and heart positions ) . Earning points for completing them.

What an awesome game! Such a simple yet unique mechanism that makes this game so much fun! More than worth your money. I would definitely recommend this for anyone looking for a challenge because the final levels will truly test your limits.

I hope they create some DLC or a sequel for this game because it honestly has endless possibilities.

I loved the game., didn't expect to be so into the story and mechanic. I think that some parts need a bit of work because sometimes the "idole" just screams although I didn't move a muscle, but the overall game is fine. In another instance objects on the table just moved and fell off the table which was annoying since it made me lose. But again, the game itself is great, I loved it.

I am already at the vampire duchess and have died to her several times because she’s got so much to do before I can kill her. I almost fell several times because of dying but I love how it gets my heart racing and the patience of this game is just magnificent!!

reviews.review_title: Good game if you suffer from vertigo
reviews.review_description: This is a great game, only on my 3rd vampire and already love it. Being that a suffer from motion sickness the devs have come up with a blinder of a game, thank you. Clever game which constantly makes you jump. 🧛‍♂️🧛‍♀️🧛🩸

I love this game so far it's exactly what it promises you in the description.The mechanics are amazing I feel like I'm really a vampire slayer tip toeing around coffins completing puzzles and disarming challenging traps. If you're looking for a fun easy to learn game that will keep you coming back I highly recommend this game.

While I'm usually an FPS player I like some puzzle games like 7th Guest. I was looking for something different that didn't require running around holding guns and Silent Slayer stood out as being something different and unique with the horror based stealth mechanics it fit the bill for something that could be played sitting or standing in one spot both in and out of the game. For once I decided to pre-order a game.

Now I've played a few levels I have to say it is overall well done and pretty much as I expected it to be. Even early puzzles can create tension and despite being pretty immune to jump scares it has still caught me out a few times. I do recommend this game but there are little niggles which can lead to frustration.

I'd like there to be some options on the controls as the default setting of using the button to interact instead of the trigger has led to several unintentional failures due to dropping the tools.

The pry bar could perhaps do with a more immersive option to lever nails without pressing a button to attach. The attach/detach cycle has a similar effect sometimes due to the way the game switches between the user's grip and automatically removing the tool from your hand, leading to more unintended errors.

Not sure why but sometimes when approaching wires with the cutters it touches them even though I'm sure they aren't out of line, maybe something to do with tracking or the stereoscopy?

There have been a few false alarms when playing. Not sure if this is a glitch or an intended barrier to completion but I've had nails just leap out of the coffin with a loud squeak even when I am being extremely careful. Another time I had removed all barriers, turned to pick up the stake, and as I turned back to face the coffin, it made the loud noise of something dropping on the ground even though there was nothing to drop and the vampire was triggered.

Finally, on one of the last levels I tried I had successfully removed all obstacles and was just turning to grab the heart and I got jumped. Apparently there actually is a time limit to completion. I am honestly struggling to understand how we are supposed to move slowly on every bar, nail, key and lever etc in order to avoid making noise, yet somehow move quickly. Even allowing for this, there is no indication of the time you have allotted to complete the puzzle. I would suggest considering introducing an optional timer to toggle the difficulty in the options menu and/or find a way to let the player know how long they have to try and beat the puzzles.

The only thing left to say is the number of puzzles seems low, so of you're pretty decent at this and don't get jumped too often it will be a short game. Presumably it will be expanded in future.

Despite the niggles it's overall an excellent game of its kind. Just be sure it's your kind of game before buying and don't expect to be running around full maps etc. Great job on this and hopefully between some minor tweaks and extra challenges it will be five stars moving forward.

developer_response.body:
Hey Slayer, here are a few tips that could help:

-Try looking at the snips from a side-on perspective. It can help with depth detection and positioning.
-Regarding the false alarm, it sounds like you might be running out of time. Try solving the levels in this order: solve all the internal traps, find and mark the hearts, break the barrier, stake the vampire. In other words, find the heart before you break the barrier.

As for a timer, you are correct, the audio cues are the only timer in the game. For more information or detailed tips, please reach out to our Player Support team at bit.ly/4aR5Wmr.

reviews.review_description: I haven’t encountered any problems with the game so far. I think its a really great game with great jump scares. Very fun to play. Only thing I would have liked to see is what others have said. Moving about the castle, disarming traps, and solving puzzles to find secret passages to other vampires. Its still a well thought up game. For $20.00 you can’t beat the fun. Looking forward to see what else can be done with this game, and the games you bring in the future. If anyone is considering buying this game based on the reviews. Just get it. Its really fun, and has a great atmosphere.

developer_response.body: Glad to hear you're enjoying the game!

reviews.review_description:
b i t , l y / v a m p k i l l e r 

As though the I expect you to die team said, "and now for something completely different" enter Silent Slayer! Cool fun, especially for those who like a bit of a scare. MR integration is very nice, though I still prefer to play in VR mode. The game definitely gets more fun the more you progress and grows on you in a great way :)

I enjoyed the game a lot however It’s a small game, I wish they had at least twice as much vampires.  A bit more variety of tools and traps would be awesome too.  The vampire’s could look more realistic with some shader / texture changes, that would make it even better.  If you want to see all vampires being slayed look for @tzanotto in YouTube.  25% off referral link on video description.
